title: HBO's 'Task' Adds 'Zero Dark Thirty' Star to Second Season of the Crime Thriller

blocks.content: HBO has an earned legacy of delivering viewers some of the best TV series across virtually every genre. While shows like The Sopranos, The Wire, and Deadwood regularly earn spots on lists that rank the best TV shows of all time, its comedy series like Curb Your Enthusiasm or Hacks earn both strong ratings and dominate awards seasons. The network has also offered up compelling mystery series like True Detective and Mare of Easttown, whose complex narratives generate buzz with the release of each new episode.

One of the pleasant surprises that HBO delivered last year was the series Task, which starred Mark Ruffalo and Tom Pelphrey. Given the pedigree involved in the show, as it was created by Mare of Easttown's Brad Inglesby, it's not a surprise that the show was compelling. Instead, it's more that Task didn't earn the same promotional push as a fantasy series like House of the Dragon, making its debut feel like a pleasant discovery. Luckily, there's still plenty more Task on the way, and it's just added another star to Season 2.

As revealed by Variety, Edgar Ramirez has joined Mark Ruffalo in Season 2 of the crime thriller. The outlet also shared that Ramirez is set to play Miguel Contreras, who is "a devoted family man and loyal DEA second-in-command torn between duty and guilt." The actor has a number of gripping projects to his name, including Zero Dark Thirty, The Assassination of Gianni Versace: American Crime Story, and The Undoing.

The first season of Task saw Ruffalo putting together a task force in rural Pennsylvania to investigate a series of drug-related robberies that escalated to murders. Season 2 has been described, "Tom Brandis (Mark Ruffalo) takes the helm of a new task force, but the deeper the operation runs, the harder it is to tell who’s the target."
In addition to Ruffalo and Ramirez, Season 2 will star Mahershala Ali, Harry Melling, Adam Nagaitis, and Aminah Nieves. Filming on Season 2 will reportedly begin this summer.

The intense series currently sits at 96% positive reviews on aggregator site Rotten Tomatoes, making it one of the best new series of 2025. Ruffalo earned himself a Best Performance by a Male Actor in a Television Series – Drama nomination at the Golden Globe Awards, and HBO submitted him for the upcoming Primetime Emmy Awards. However, he'll likely be going up against another HBO star, as Noah Wyle won that category for his work on The Pitt last year.
With Task not shooting until this summer, we shouldn't expect it to premiere until sometime in 2027.
